title: Bar-Seq Library Preparation and Pooling: Preparation of Sera-Mag SpeedBeads
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4ecom","text":"This protocol describes the preparation of Sera-mag SpeedBeads with PEG-salt buffer for downstream use in the Bar-Seq Library Preparation and Pooling protocols (see the Automation Instructions \u0026 User Instructions).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":169,"length":23},{"key":1,"offset":195,"length":17}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"7496FBD07EA84AC9AB49E7CF07833A9C","url":"http://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.3byl49qdjgo5/v4"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"8CBFADADAEFE41D1BBA2A44577A8BFE5","url":"http://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.81wgbrzo1lpk/v1"}}}}

mL of Sera-mag SpeedBeads Solution (MilliporeSigma 65152105050250)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2mq8f","text":"15 mL of TE individual 2 mL microcentrifuge tubes (Celltreat 229446)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1vtvb","text":"50 mL sterile conical tubes (ThermoFisher 339652)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Vortex 15 mL Sera-mag SpeedBeads solution until beads are uniformly distributed.
Place all 15 of the tubes on the tube magnetic separation rack until beads are drawn to magnet.
Remove the supernatant from each tube.
Transfer 1 mL to each of the fifteen 2 mL microcentrifuge tubes.
Add 1 mL TE buffer to each tube.
Remove the tubes from the magnetic stand and mix by vortexing
Return tubes to the magnetic separation rack and wait until beads are drawn to magnet.
Remove supernatant from each tube.
Add 1 mL TE buffer to each tube.
Remove the tubes from the magnetic stand and mix by vortexing
Return tubes to the magnetic separation rack and wait until beads are drawn to magnet.
Remove supernatant from each tube.
Add 1 mL TE to each tube and remove from magnet.

Fully resuspend by vortexing and set in a non-magnetic tube rack (not on magnetic stand). Note: Beads that have been washed and resuspended in TE can be stored in the refrigerator at this point and used for the following steps as needed.
Wrap in the conical tube in foil (or place in dark container) and store at 4 °C until use.
Gently mix until the solution is a uniformed brown color.
Fill the conical tube to the 50 mL mark with sterile nuclease free water
Mix two 1 mL aliquots of the SpeedBeads + TE solution until the solution is a uniform brown color, then transfer the solutions from both tubes to the 50 mL conical tube.
Add 27.5 μL Tween 20 to the conical tube and mix gently by inverting 4-6 times.

Mix the conical tube by vortexing every 1-2 minutes until PEG goes into solution. When not vortexing, the tube can be placed on a heated shaker (e.g., Eppendorf ThermoMixer) at 50°C, 600 rpm, to reduce time required to dissolve the PEG When finished, the solution should be clear.
Fill the conical tube to ~45 mL using sterile nuclease free water.
Add 500 µL 0.5 mol/L EDTA to the conical tube.
Add 500 µL 1 mol/L Tris-HCl, pH8 to the conical tube.
Add 10 mL 5 mol/L NaCl to the conical tube.
Add 9 g PEG-8000 to a 50 mL sterile conical tube.
creator: Open Datasets Initiative
